sector.name: PEB
sector.description: PEB and prefab players serving industrial and infrastructure capex

fyPerformance: FY26 Revenue: ₹1,898 Cr (+31% YoY); EBITDA: ₹176 Cr (+29% YoY); PAT: ₹135 Cr (+25% YoY)
Order book stood at ₹1,703 Cr as of April 2026
Export orders worth over ₹40 Cr were received in FY26
Spent ₹127 Cr in capex to expand capacities in Andhra Pradesh, Kiccha and Gujarat

guidance.value: ₹3,500 Cr after all capacities come live

highlights: Andhra Pradesh expansion is focused on heavy steel structures
Gujarat PEB plant is located in a major industrial cluster and targets sectors like EVs and semiconductors
Company secured export certifications for Canada and USA
MoU signed with ER Steel for structural steel and PEB business across Canada and North America
Sector tailwinds remain strong from industrial capex, warehousing, logistics, renewable energy, data centres, semiconductors and large manufacturing projects

fyPerformance: FY26 Revenue: ₹1,525 Cr (+35% YoY); EBITDA: ₹160 Cr (+36% YoY); PAT: ₹93 Cr (+56% YoY)
Prefab business grew around 45% YoY in FY26
Capacity utilisation improved through the year and reached 83% in Q4FY26
Operating cash flow stood at ₹136 Cr, around 85% of EBITDA; net cash stood at ₹201 Cr
highlights: Management believes 30% growth is achievable in the prefab division based on order book, pipeline and capacity additions
Order book and pipeline provide strong FY27 visibility
Higher capacity utilisation through FY26 indicates operating leverage potential
Healthy operating cash conversion and net cash position support the next phase of growth
Structural shift from conventional construction to faster, factory-made and execution-efficient solutions remains a key demand driver

fyPerformance: FY26 Revenue: ₹1,260 Cr (+27% YoY); PAT: ₹93 Cr (+20% YoY)
Unexecuted order book stood at ₹1,083 Cr, growing 35% YoY
FY26 order inflow stood at ₹1,539 Cr (+28% YoY)
Phenix division FY26 revenue stood at ₹985 Cr (+29% YoY); Proflex division revenue stood at ₹275 Cr (+23% YoY)
highlights: Management has not given specific margin guidance due to uncertainty in steel prices, freight, FX and geopolitical conditions
FY26 capex was ₹33 Cr, while FY27 capex is expected around ₹100 Cr
Company is adding around 20,000 MT capacity at Sanand, expected to be commissioned in Q2FY27
Export revenue is targeted to rise sharply to ₹275-300 Cr in FY27 from ₹165 Cr in FY26
Margin pressure in Q4 was due to FX loss, input cost inflation and freight costs
